torch_pattern: x = x.permute(0, 2, 1)  # (N, C, L) -> (N, L, C) channels-last; out = torch.nn.functional.conv1d(x_ncl, weight, bias, stride={STRIDE}, padding={PADDING}, dilation={DILATION}); out = out.permute(0, 2, 1)
weight_transform: torch Conv1d weight of shape (out_channels, in_channels/groups, kernel_size) is passed host-side in ROW_MAJOR layout (no pre-tilization); ttnn.conv1d preprocesses it on-device on first call and returns the prepared weight ([new_w, new_b]) which should be cached and reused on subsequent calls (e.g. inside trace capture). Bias is passed as a ROW_MAJOR tensor (reshaped to (1, 1, 1, out_channels)). Activation must be reshaped to (batch, 1, input_length, in_channels) channels-last ROW_MAJOR.
applicability_notes: torch_pattern_notes: ttnn.conv1d consumes the activation in channels-last (N, 1, L, C) ROW_MAJOR form; call sites reshape via `x_rm = ttnn.reshape(x_rm, (batch, 1, seq_len, in_channels))` before the call. The op replaces the contiguous torch subsequence permute(NCL->NLC-equiv) -> F.conv1d -> permute back. Returns (output, out_length) or (output, out_length, [prepared_weight, prepared_bias]) when return_weights_and_bias is set.

placement_observations.source: call_site: '# ttnn.conv1d wants ROW_MAJOR input; convert if needed.' followed by ttnn.to_layout(y_dw_in, ttnn.ROW_MAJOR_LAYOUT); also ttnn.sharded_to_interleaved(input_embeds) before conv1d in Whisper conv2 split path
call_site docstring: 'weight_memory_config: where to place weights (default DRAM) ... the conv1/conv2 weights are kept in row-major as required by ttnn.conv1d (it preprocesses them on first call)'
test_example: override_sharding_config branch setting num_cores_nhw to 98

placement_observations.program_config: batch loop: process one batch slice at a time with batch_size=1
placement_observations.source: call_site: '# TODO: This is a hack to support batch size > 1, till the Conv OOM issue is resolved' — per-batch slicing loop in Whisper conv1

placement_observations.size_descriptor: out_channels split into channels_split_factor groups (channel-split to avoid OOM)
placement_observations.program_config: horizontal channel split: run conv1d per weight split (in_channels // channels_split_factor, out_channels // channels_split_factor) and concat outputs
placement_observations.source: call_site: loop over self.config.channels_split_factor with self.tt_weight_tensor_splits[i]
